skill.name: web-artifacts-builder
skill.description: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components - not for simple single-file HTML/JSX artifacts.
skill.summary: Build professional React + TypeScript + shadcn/ui artifacts bundled into single HTML files for Claude conversations.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 39 patterns across 3 files (451 lines). All findings evaluated as false positives: shell commands are legitimate build tooling with hardcoded arguments, filesystem operations modify only project-local configuration files, network references are documentation URLs only, and weak crypto detections are false matches on CSS color values. The skill is a frontend scaffolding tool with no malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Shell Script Execution
low_findings.description: Build scripts execute shell commands for project scaffolding. All commands use hardcoded arguments with no user input injection vectors. Standard pattern for development tooling.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Modification
low_findings.description: Scripts modify project configuration files (tsconfig.json, tailwind.config.js) using Node.js fs module. Operations are scoped to the created project directory only.

content.user_title: Build React Artifacts with shadcn/ui Components
content.value_statement: Creating multi-component React artifacts for Claude conversations requires complex bundling and configuration. This skill automates project setup with React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and 40+ shadcn/ui components, then bundles everything into a single HTML file.
actual_capabilities: Initialize React 18 + TypeScript projects with Vite configured for Node 18+ compatibility
Install and configure Tailwind CSS 3.4.1 with shadcn/ui theming system and path aliases
Pre-install 40+ shadcn/ui components including Radix UI dependencies
Bundle complete React applications into single self-contained HTML files using Parcel
Generate production-ready artifacts suitable for sharing in Claude conversations
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or higher and pnpm package manager
Designed for multi-component artifacts, not simple single-file HTML/JSX outputs
Requires a local development environment with shell script execution capability
Bundled HTML size may be large due to inlined React and component libraries

use_cases.title: Interactive Dashboard Components
use_cases.description: Build complex data visualization dashboards with multiple shadcn/ui components like tables, cards, charts, and filters that work directly in Claude conversations.

use_cases.title: Multi-Step Form Applications
use_cases.description: Create sophisticated form flows with validation, state management, and UI components like dialogs, tabs, and accordions for user onboarding experiences.

use_cases.title: Design System Showcases
use_cases.description: Generate comprehensive component libraries showcasing shadcn/ui theming, dark mode support, and responsive layouts for design documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Start - Initialize Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Initialize a new web artifact project called "dashboard-demo" with all shadcn/ui components configured.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new artifact project from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Build a Data Table with Filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a customer management dashboard with a data table showing name, email, and status. Add filter controls using the select and input components. Include pagination and row actions via dropdown menu.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Form with Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a 3-step registration form using the form, input, select, and button components. Use react-hook-form with zod validation. Step 1 collects personal info, Step 2 handles preferences, Step 3 shows confirmation. Include progress indicator.

prompt_templates.title: Dark Mode Analytics Dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an analytics dashboard with dark mode toggle using next-themes. Include metric cards, a line chart placeholder, recent activity table, and a settings dialog. Use the card, table, dialog, button, and switch components with proper shadcn/ui theming.

output_examples.input: Initialize a project called "task-board" with kanban board layout
output_examples.output: Project 'task-board' created successfully with React 18, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS 3.4.1, and 40+ shadcn/ui components. Path aliases (@/) configured. Run 'cd task-board && pnpm dev' to start development server.

output_examples.input: Bundle the current React project into a single HTML artifact
output_examples.output: Bundle complete! Output: bundle.html (487 KB). All JavaScript, CSS, and assets inlined. Open bundle.html in browser to preview, or share directly in Claude conversations.

best_practices: Always run init-artifact.sh before starting development to ensure all dependencies and configurations are properly set up
Use the provided path aliases (@/components, @/lib) for cleaner imports consistent with shadcn/ui conventions
Run bundle-artifact.sh only after development is complete to minimize build time and output size
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for simple single-file React snippets - it is designed for multi-component applications
Avoid modifying the build scripts unless you understand Parcel bundling configuration and path alias resolution
Do not commit the bundle.html to version control - generate it fresh for each artifact sharing session

faq.question: What is the difference between this skill and simple React artifact generation?
faq.answer: This skill creates full multi-component React applications with proper build tooling, while simple artifacts are single-file JSX snippets. Use this for complex UIs requiring state management, routing, or many shadcn/ui components.

faq.question: Can I use npm or yarn instead of pnpm?
faq.answer: The scripts are written for pnpm. You can manually adapt the commands for npm or yarn, but pnpm is recommended for compatibility with the provided scripts and faster dependency installation.

faq.question: Why is the bundled HTML file so large?
faq.answer: The bundle includes the entire React library, all shadcn/ui components, and Tailwind CSS inlined into a single file. This trade-off enables sharing as a self-contained artifact without external dependencies.

faq.question: Can I add custom shadcn/ui components after initialization?
faq.answer: Yes. The components.json configuration is set up for the shadcn/ui CLI. Run 'pnpm dlx shadcn-ui@latest add <component>' to add more components to your project.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude Code workspace mode?
faq.answer: Yes. The scripts operate within the current project directory. When using Claude Code, ensure you run commands from the artifact project root where package.json exists.

faq.question: How do I customize the Tailwind theme beyond the default shadcn colors?
faq.answer: Edit tailwind.config.js to add custom colors, fonts, or extend the theme. The config is pre-configured with shadcn CSS variables that you can modify or extend.
